content.text:
```mdl
@ CNDA – Confidentiality and Non‑Disclosure Agreement
module contract_nli_no_align_dev_10_nda_13

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain model
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
type Party =
    | DisclosingParty()
    | ReceivingParty()

type ConfidentialInfo = {
    source: Party,               # who supplied the information
    content: string,             # the actual data (abstracted)
    marked_confidential: bool    # true if the document is clearly marked “Confidential”
}

type ThirdPartyInfo = {
    source: string,              # name of the third‑party source
    content: string,             # the actual data (abstracted)
    marked_confidential: bool    # true if the third‑party document is marked “Confidential”
}

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Entities (public model objects)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
entity disclosing_party: Party = Party.DisclosingParty()
entity receiving_party: Party = Party.ReceivingParty()
entity confidential_info: ConfidentialInfo
entity third_party_info: ThirdPartyInfo

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper functions
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Determines whether a piece of information is “Confidential Information” as
# defined in Article 2.01 of the CNDA.
func is_confidential(info: ConfidentialInfo) -> bool:
    info.marked_confidential or info.source = Party.DisclosingParty()

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Requirements (deontic rules)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2.03 – The Receiving Party must not disclose Confidential Information.
rule F cannot_disclose_confidential:
    is_confidential(confidential_info) always

# 2.04 – The Receiving Party may acquire information that is *similar* to
# Confidential Information from a third party, provided that such information
# is not itself marked confidential.  This captures the hypothesis that
# “Receiving Party may acquire information similar to Confidential Information
# from a third party.”
rule P acquire_third_party_info:
    not third_party_info.marked_confidential always

# 2.05 – The Receiving Party must not disclose third‑party information that
# is marked confidential (i.e. it becomes Confidential Information under the
# definition above).
rule F cannot_disclose_third_party_confidential:
    third_party_info.marked_confidential always

# 2.07 – Upon request, the Receiving Party must return or destroy all
# Confidential Information.  (Simplified to an obligation that the condition
# holds at every step.)
rule O return_confidential_on_request:
    true always
```
